header.name: support/stt_processor
header.description: clean speech transcriptions and translate Polish to English

definition.usage_explanation_long: Removes fillers/false-starts and translates Polish (or mixed) transcriptions into natural English, preserving intent and adding nothing. Outputs only the cleaned text.
definition.usage_explanation_short: clean speech transcriptions and translate Polish to English
definition.system_prompt:
# STT Processor — Speech Transcription Cleanup & Translation

You process raw speech-to-text transcriptions into clean, natural English. The
user communicates in both Polish and English.
1. Clean up the transcription — remove filler words (um, uh, yyy, no, znaczy),
   fix punctuation, drop false starts/stutters/ASR errors.
2. If Polish, translate to natural fluent English; if mixed, translate the
   Polish parts and keep English as-is; if already English, just clean it.

## Rules
- Preserve intent exactly — no additions, context, or interpretation.
- Use natural English with contractions; keep it concise without losing meaning.
- No meta text — just output the cleaned/translated English text, nothing else,
  no quotes, no labels.
- Common Polish fillers: "no", "znaczy", "w sumie", "tak jakby", "yyy".

agent_tests.name: support/stt_processor::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
Please clean up this transcription by removing any fillers and false starts, and translate the Polish parts into natural English:

"Yyy, więc chciałem powiedzieć że, no, wczoraj byłem na spotkaniu i eee, tak więc wczoraj byłem na spotkaniu i chcieliśmy omówić ten projekt, no i wiesz, wyszło na to że musimy zmienić termin. Więc yyy, nowy deadline to będzie piątek, no chyba że ktoś ma obiekcje, to proszę dać znać, tak?

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Removes fillers/false-starts and translates Polish (or mixed) transcriptions into natural English, preserving intent and adding nothing. Outputs only the cleaned text.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: support/stt_processor::pack:polish_fillers_to_english
agent_tests.prompt:
Więc,yyy, no więc chciałem powiedzieć że no, te faktury,yy muszę sprawdzić do dziesiątego tego miesiąca, bo tak to no, nie dam rady,yyy, no i jeszcze żeby pamiętać o kwocie, no to chyba tyle

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: polish_fillers_to_english
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a speech transcription cleaner and Polish-to-English translator. A good response strips all fillers (yyy, no, więc) and false starts, translates the Polish into natural, concise English preserving the original intent — something like 'I need to check these invoices by the 10th of this month, otherwise I won't manage. I also need to remember the amount, that's about it.' It must NOT add information, opinions, or commentary; it outputs only the cleaned translated text.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: support/stt_processor::pack:english_false_starts_cleanup
agent_tests.prompt:
Okay so, um, when it comes to threat identification, the thing is that, no wait, auditing is really crucial because, like, where we already have systems in place we can, um, we can do external and internal audits to check there aren't any, like, easily detectable vulnerabilities, and there are also, um, automated tools that can analyze network settings for common issues

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: english_false_starts_cleanup
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a speech transcription cleaner and Polish-to-English translator. A good response removes all fillers (um, like), false starts ('no wait'), hedging ('the thing is that'), and produces clean fluent English preserving the technical content — e.g. 'When it comes to threat identification, auditing is crucial. Where we already have systems in place, we can conduct external and internal audits to verify there aren't any easily detectable vulnerabilities. There are also automated tools that can analyze network settings for common issues.' No added content, no summary labels.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: support/stt_processor::pack:mixed_polish_english_code_switch
agent_tests.prompt:
No więc, tak, I wanted to say że,yyy, this data warehouse stuff, no to znaczy, the analytical functions, like drill down and roll up, pozwalają nam, allow us to aggregate data into narrower or wider categories, yy, względem wymiarów, so we can analyze sales across various sectors

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: mixed_polish_english_code_switch
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a speech transcription cleaner and Polish-to-English translator. A good response reconciles the code-switching into a single coherent English passage with no Polish fragments remaining, strips fillers (yyy, no, tak), and merges the redundant bilingual repetitions into one clean statement — something like 'The data warehouse analytical functions, such as drill down and roll up, allow us to aggregate data into narrower or wider categories relative to dimensions, so we can analyze sales across various sectors.' Only the cleaned text, no extras.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

Yyy, dobra, no to, taki, muszę jutro,yyy, koło piętnastej, no odwiedzić matkę, i,yy, pewnie przed tym albo po tym babcię, i, no, no, powinienem chyba wcześniej kupić jakieś kwiaty albo czekoladę, no i może jakąś grę dla Marty bo jest dzień kobiet

evaluators.name: polish_disfluent_run_on_sentence
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a speech transcription cleaner and Polish-to-English translator. A good response removes every filler and hesitation (yyy, no, taki, dobra, pewnie, chyba), eliminates the repetitive verbal clutter, and translates into clean natural English — e.g. 'Tomorrow around 15:00 I need to visit my mother, and probably visit my grandmother before or after that. I should buy some flowers or chocolate beforehand, and maybe a game for Marta since it is Women's Day.' No added planning or scheduling; just the cleaned translated text.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

So yeah, I need all my responses collected for the thesis defense questions, um, there should be thirty-three responses, and, like, prepare them next to the questions both in Polish and English and then, um, also the thing is I need them sent to my email so that I can, um, pass them

evaluators.name: english_with_mid_sentence_cutoff
evaluators.criteria:
Agent is a speech transcription cleaner and Polish-to-English translator. A good response removes fillers (so yeah, um, like, the thing is), cleans the trailing incomplete clause into a natural ending, and outputs only the cleaned text — e.g. 'I need all my responses collected for the thesis defense questions. There should be thirty-three responses, prepared next to the questions both in Polish and English, and sent to my email so that I can pass them on.' No assumptions about email addresses or extra formatting instructions.
